name: California Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in California — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

mainEntity.name: Does California require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, California requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in California.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: State application fees are $120. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
